sentence: Subjective:

HPI: Stephanie Morales, a 36-year-old female, presents with complaints of ankle pain after slipping on ice two days ago, resulting in a twisting injury to her left ankle, which is more on the outside of her ankle.
She reports falling and being unable to walk due to significant pain and swelling.
She has tried ibuprofen, ice, and elevation for pain and swelling, with minimal relief.
The pain is localized to the outside of her ankle, with no pain radiating to her calf, lower leg, or knee.
She denies bleeding.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of diabetes and high blood pressure.
She is taking metformin and norvasc.
Her most recent hemoglobin A1c was 6.8, and she is due for an eye exam for her diabetes checkup.
Review of systems: The patient denies any other symptoms.
Current medications:
Metformin [dose not mentioned] [frequency not mentioned] for diabetes
Norvasc [dose not mentioned] [frequency not mentioned] for high blood pressure
Objective:
Vital signs: Blood pressure 120/70.
Physical exam: The patient's neck exam is normal, with no bruits.
Her lungs are clear, and her heart exam is normal, with a 2/6 systolic ejection murmur that is unchanged from before.
Her belly exam is normal.
The ankle exam reveals tenderness over the lateral malleolus and the fifth metatarsal, with swelling, redness, and pain with valgus stressing of the ankle.
Her pulses are normal, and she has normal sensation.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has an ankle sprain, and an x-ray is ordered to rule out a fracture.
Problem list: Ankle sprain; diabetes; high blood pressure; suspected fracture (status: suspected).
Plan:
I ordered an x-ray of the patient's ankle to rule out a fracture.
I placed her in an air splint and gave her crutches to avoid weightbearing until the x-ray results are available.
I prescribed naprosyn 500mg twice a day for pain control.
I instructed her to keep the ankle elevated, apply ice, and monitor for any changes.
If the x-ray shows a fracture, I will refer her to orthopedics.
Follow-up: The patient will return for a follow-up appointment to discuss the x-ray results and adjust her treatment plan as needed.
